doc.title: Lessee Operating Lease
doc.event: Kerr Co. leases office space for 3 years at $30,000 per year, payable at each year-end. Kerr's 6% incremental borrowing rate applies, and the present value of the payments is $80,190. The lease is classified as operating. Prepare Kerr's entry at commencement and the combined entry for the first year-end payment.

entries.label: Commencement — same setup as a finance lease

lines.why: Operating leases STILL go on the balance sheet — ROU and liability at PV.
lines.trap: The old 'operating = off balance sheet' rule is dead. Classification changes the income statement, not the balance sheet.

entries.label: Year-end payment — one straight-line expense (interest of $4,811 is embedded)

lines.why: Operating lessee reports ONE straight-line lease expense — no separate interest line.
Liability drops by payment minus embedded interest: 30,000 − (6% × 80,190) = 30,000 − 4,811.
lines.trap: Reducing the liability by the full 30,000 ignores the interest accretion.

lines.why: ROU amortization is the PLUG: lease expense 30,000 − interest 4,811 = 25,189.

memorize.body: Operating lessee: same balance sheet as finance (ROU + liability), but the income statement shows ONE straight-line lease expense.
ROU amortization in an operating lease is a plug — lease expense minus interest accretion. It changes every year even though total expense is flat.

questions.prompt: In year 1, how much INTEREST EXPENSE does Kerr report separately on its income statement?

distractors.feedback: The 4,811 of interest is embedded inside the single lease expense line — operating lessees never show it separately.
30,000 is total lease expense (which happens to equal cash here) — but the question asked for a separate INTEREST line, and there isn't one.

questions.prompt: By how much does Kerr's lease liability decrease in year 1?

distractors.feedback: Part of the payment covers the 6% interest accretion (4,811). Only the remainder reduces the liability.
You flipped it — 4,811 is the interest piece; the liability falls by the OTHER piece, 25,189.

build.scaffold: Commencement mirrors a finance lease. The year-end entry has FOUR lines and no Interest Expense account — the ROU credit is the plug.
